title: Vowels Part 3: Special Sounds
description: You've learned 11 vowels so far (4 short + 7 long). Now meet the final two special vowel modifiers: अं (Anusvara) and अः (Visarga). These aren't pure vowels — they modify the vowel sound by adding nasalization or a breathy 'h' at the end. The Anusvara (अं) nasalizes the vowel (like saying it through your nose), and the Visarga (अः) adds a soft 'h' breath after the vowel.
keyPoints: Anusvara (अं) — adds a nasal 'm/n' sound after the vowel
Visarga (अः) — adds a soft 'h' breath after the vowel
These complete the 13 vowels of the Hindi alphabet
Anusvara is very common in everyday Hindi words

title: Anusvara and Visarga
explanation: The Anusvara (shown as a dot above: ं) nasalizes the preceding vowel. Think of the 'n' in 'hint' or 'm' in 'jump' — the sound goes through your nose. The Visarga (shown as two dots after: ः) adds a breathy 'h' after the vowel, like a gentle exhale. Anusvara is extremely common (हिंदी, मंदिर, अंगूर), while Visarga appears mostly in Sanskrit-origin words (दुःख, प्रातःकाल).
simpleRule: ं (Anusvara) = nasal 'm/n' sound (dot above). ः (Visarga) = soft breath 'h' (two dots after). Together with the 11 vowels, these make 13 total.

vietnamese: an — âm mũi hóa (Anusvara), như 'n' trong 'hunt'

english: ah — aspirated breath (Visarga), a soft 'h' after the vowel
vietnamese: ah — hơi thở nhẹ (Visarga), âm 'h' nhẹ sau nguyên âm

memoryTrick: The famous Hindi fable: 'अंगूर खट्टे हैं' (The grapes are sour) — like Aesop's fox!
You're learning हिंदी right now! Notice the Anusvara dot (ं) on the ह — it nasalizes to 'hin'.
MAN-dir — think 'a MAN goes to the DIR(ection of the) temple'. The ं nasalizes 'ma' to 'man'.
DUKH — the Visarga (ः) adds a breathy pause between 'du' and 'kh'. Feel the sigh of sadness!

prompt: Which symbol represents the Anusvara (nasal sound)?
text: अनुस्वार (नासिक ध्वनि) का चिह्न कौन सा है?

subtext: Look for the two vertical dots ः

instruction: Match all 13 Hindi vowels with their romanized sounds!
Match the remaining vowels and special sounds!

title: All 13 Vowels — Complete Summary
takeaways: Short vowels: अ (a), इ (i), उ (u), ऋ (ri)
Long vowels: आ (aa), ई (ee), ऊ (oo), ए (e), ऐ (ai), ओ (o), औ (au)
Special modifiers: अं (Anusvara — nasal), अः (Visarga — breathy 'h')
Anusvara (ं) is very common: हिंदी, मंदिर, अंगूर
Visarga (ः) appears in Sanskrit-origin words: दुःख, प्रातःकाल
You've now learned ALL Hindi vowels! 🎉 Next: consonants!
